name: Pumauna Waterfalls
description: Pumauna Waterfalls is one of Tagum's most breathtaking natural attractions, featuring a spectacular cascade of water surrounded by lush tropical vegetation. The multi-tiered waterfalls create a serene and picturesque setting perfect for nature lovers and photographers.
location: Located approximately 30km north of Tagum City center
accessibility: 45 minutes by car from the city, then 30-minute trek through forest
features:
Three-tiered waterfall system
Natural pools for swimming
Scenic picnic areas
facilities:
Basic restrooms
Picknic benches
Changing rooms
best_time:
December to May (dry season) - Best water visibility
June to August - Some rainfall, lush vegetation
Avoid: September to November (typhoon season)
Best Time: Early morning from 7am to 10am
what_to_pack:
Comfortable hiking shoes with good grip
Swimwear and water-resistant bag
Sunscreen (SPF 50+) and insect repellent
Hat or cap for sun protection
2-3 liters of drinking water
Light snacks
Quick-dry towel
Basic first aid kit
Waterproof phone case
Trash bags
visiting_rules:
Do not litter - carry all waste out
Stay on marked trails only
No diving from rocks
Swimming in designated areas only
Do not use chemical sunscreen
Do not pick flowers or plants
Do not feed animals
Parental supervision required for children

name: Azuela Springs
description: Azuela Springs offers pristine natural pools with crystal clear water fed by underground springs. The cool, refreshing waters and beautiful surroundings make it an ideal spot for relaxation and recreation.
location: Located 20km southeast of Tagum City center
accessibility: 30 minutes by car from the city, easy walking access
features:
Multiple natural pools
Underground spring source
Adjacent restaurant and shops
facilities:
Restrooms
Changing rooms
Picknic areas
Food vendors
Parking lot
best_time:
December to May - Clear skies and pleasant weather
Weekdays - Less crowded than weekends
Morning Hours: 8am to 12pm
Avoid: September to November
what_to_pack:
Swimwear and cover-up clothing
Sunscreen (SPF 50+)
Sunglasses and hat
Water shoes or flip-flops
Towel
Cash for purchases
Reusable water bottle
Camera
visiting_rules:
Keep the springs clean
Do not use soap in water
Do not use non-biodegradable sunscreen
Respect maximum capacity limits
Do not disturb the spring ecosystem
Secure your belongings
No alcoholic beverages
No glass bottles

name: Mt. Kampalilis
description: Mt. Kampalilis stands as one of the region's most majestic peaks, offering challenging hiking trails through pristine forests with panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. The mountain is home to diverse flora and fauna.
features:
Mountain peak views (1,240m)
Old-growth forests
Endemics species
Alpine meadows
facilities:
Ranger station at base
Basic shelter at summit
best_time:
December to April - Dry weather
Thursday to Sunday recommended
Start at 5:00 AM for summit by noon
Avoid: June to October (heavy rains)
Full Moon nights popular for night hikes
what_to_pack:
Sturdy hiking boots
Light jacket or sweater
Rain jacket
Long-sleeve shirt
3-4 liters of water
Energy bars and snacks
Sunscreen and hat
First aid kit
Headlamp or flashlight
Rope or trekking poles
Map and compass
Insect repellent
visiting_rules:
Guide is mandatory
Register at ranger station
Report itinerary to family
Stay on marked trails
No hiking after sunset without guide
No hunting or trapping
Do not collect plants
No campfires
Practice Leave No Trace

name: Tagum River
description: The Tagum River winds through lush valleys and provides numerous opportunities for water-based activities. Its crystal-clear waters and scenic surroundings make it perfect for both adventure seekers and nature enthusiasts.
location: Flows through central Tagum City with multiple access points
accessibility: Multiple viewing spots with varying access levels
features:
River fishing opportunities
Boating and swimming areas
Photography locations
Birdwatching spots
facilities: Vary by location - some have parking, picnic areas, restrooms
best_time:
December to May - Calm waters and clear visibility
June to August - Moderate water level
September to November - High water (dangerous)
Early morning best for wildlife spotting
Golden hours best for photography
what_to_pack:
Life jacket if boating
Water shoes
Swimwear
Sunscreen and lip balm
Hat and sunglasses
Quick-dry shirt and shorts
Towel
Drinking water
Snacks
Fishing gear (if applicable)
Binoculars
Camera
visiting_rules:
Stay clear of strong currents
Do not swim alone
Wear life jacket when boating
Do not pollute the river
Respect private property
Do not feed animals
Follow fishing regulations
Do not disturb nesting birds
Stay out during heavy rains

name: Historical Museum
description: The Tagum City Museum preserves and showcases the rich cultural heritage and historical artifacts of the city. From pre-colonial artifacts to modern memorabilia, the museum tells the story of Tagum's past and present.
location: City Hall Complex, downtown Tagum City
accessibility: Central location, easy access from downtown
features:
Pre-colonial artifacts
Historical documents
Cultural exhibits
Modern displays
facilities:
Restrooms
Gift shop
Guide services
Parking
best_time:
Monday to Friday - Less crowded
Morning Hours: 9am to 12pm optimal
Avoid: Public holidays and festivals
Indoor activity - suitable for rainy days
what_to_pack:
Comfortable walking shoes
Notebook and pen
Camera (if allowed)
Water bottle
Light jacket
Reading glasses
Hand sanitizer
Cash or card
Small bag for pamphlets
visiting_rules:
Do not touch artifacts
No flash photography
Keep voices low
No eating except designated areas
Do not lean on displays
Supervise children
Do not remove items
Use restrooms beforehand
Report broken displays
Respect cultural sensitivity

name: San Fernando Church
description: San Fernando de Tagum Church is a masterpiece of Spanish colonial architecture, dating back several centuries. This religious and cultural landmark represents the deep faith and rich history of the Tagum community.
features:
Spanish colonial baroque architecture
Stone facade
Wooden interior
Bell tower
Stained-glass windows
Historic altar
facilities:
Limited facilities
Accessible during service hours
best_time:
Visit outside of mass times
Mid-morning (10-11 AM) for good light
Late afternoon for architectural photography
Weekday afternoons quieter
Avoid peak service times
what_to_pack:
Modest clothing (cover shoulders and knees)
Comfortable walking shoes
Hat or head covering
Camera (for external shots)
Notebook for sketches
Respectful attitude
visiting_rules:
Dress modestly
Remove shoes if instructed
Maintain silence
No flash photography inside
Do not disrupt services
Do not touch altar or artifacts
Do not sit on pews during services
Follow staff instructions
Show respect for worshippers
No eating or smoking inside

name: Energy Park (Epark)
description: A park is a designated public space developed to provide a safe and relaxing environment for recreation, leisure, and community activities. It allows visitors to enjoy nature, participate in outdoor activities, and promote social interaction within the community.
location: CR8F+7VW, Unnamed Road, Tagum, Davao del Norte
accessibility: Wheelchair accessible entrance and Wheelchair accessible parking lot
features:
Picnics
Good for kids
Good for kids birthday
Playground
Dogs allowed
facilities:
Baseball field
Basketball court
Cycling lanes
Has a tennis court
Picnic tables
Public restroom
Skateboarding area
Slides
Swings
Volleyball court
best_time: Every Week at 3:00 pm to 5:00 pm
visiting_rules:
1.Keep the Park Clean

Dispose of trash properly in the designated bins.

Do not litter in the park.

Help maintain a clean and pleasant environment for everyone.

2. Respect Nature

Do not damage plants, trees, or flowers.

Avoid picking or removing plants from the park.

Protect wildlife and do not disturb animals.

3. Maintain Peace and Order

Keep noise levels low to respect other visitors.

Loud music and disruptive behavior are not allowed.

Follow instructions from park staff or authorities.

4. Safety First

Children must be supervised by adults at all times.

Use park facilities and equipment properly.

Report any hazards or accidents to park management immediately.

5. Proper Use of Facilities

Use benches, playgrounds, and other facilities responsibly.

Do not vandalize or damage park property.

Follow posted signs for specific areas and activities.

6. Pets Policy

Pets must be kept on a leash at all times.

Owners must clean up after their pets.

Ensure pets do not disturb other visitors.

7. Prohibited Activities

Smoking, drinking alcohol, or illegal activities are strictly prohibited.

Do not bring dangerous objects or substances into the park.

Commercial activities require prior permission from park management.

8. Park Operating Hours

Visitors must follow the official park opening and closing hours.

Staying in the park after closing time is not allowed.

name: Nabintad River Side Park
description: Nabintad River in Tagum City is known for its clear, refreshing waters and lush surroundings. It offers a peaceful and calming atmosphere for visitors. The gentle flow of the river makes it ideal for relaxing and enjoying nature. It is a popular spot for swimming and simple outdoor bonding. Overall,
accessibility: 45 minutes by car from the city, then 30-minute trek through forest
best_time: Early morning or late afternoon for cooler weather and fewer crowds. Dry season (November–May) is ideal for clearer water and safer conditions.
what_to_pack: Swimwear, extra clothes, towel, drinking water, snacks, sunscreen, and slippers or aqua shoes.
visiting_rules: Keep the area clean bring your trash with you. Avoid loud noise to preserve the peaceful environment. Follow safety precautions and respect local guidelines.
